title: 360Â° video viewer control using smart device
abstract: An electronic device includes a touchscreen display and at least one processor. The at least one processor is configured to present a dynamic user interface on the touchscreen display. The dynamic user interface includes a first input region forming a dial. The first input region is configured to receive user input related to a first viewing direction associated with a 360Â° video. The at least one processor is also configured to detect the user input. In addition, the at least one processor is configured to transmit the user input to a display device to control presentation of the 360Â° video on the display device. The dynamic user interface may further include a second input region, where the second input region is configured to receive second user input related to a second viewing direction associated with the 360Â° video.
claims: Claims ( 15 ) An electronic device comprising: a touchscreen display; and at least one processor configured to: present a dynamic user interface on the touchscreen display, the dynamic user interface comprising a first input region forming a dial, the first input region configured to receive user input related to a first viewing direction associated with a 360Â° video; detect the user input; and transmit the user input to a display device to control presentation of the 360Â° video on the display device. The electronic device of Claim 1, wherein: the dynamic user interface further comprises a dynamic heat map; and the dynamic heat map indicates at least one position of desired content in the 360Â° video. The electronic device of Claim 1, wherein: the user input is detected using one of: an absolute mode or a relative mode; in the absolute mode, changes to the first viewing direction are made with reference to a fixed viewing direction; and in the relative mode, changes to the first viewing direction are made with reference to a current viewing direction. The electronic device of Claim 1, wherein: the dynamic user interface further comprises a second input region, the second input region configured to receive second user input related to a second viewing direction associated with the 360Â° video; and the at least one processor is further configured to: detect the second user input; and transmit the second user input to the display device to further control the presentation of the 360Â° video on the display device. The electronic device of Claim 1, further comprising at least one haptic motor; wherein the at least one processor is further configured to control the at least one haptic motor based on a position of desired content in the 360Â° video. A display device comprising: a transceiver; a display configured to present a 360Â° video; and at least one processor configured to: receive user input from a dynamic user interface on a touchscreen of an electronic device via the transceiver, the user input related to a first viewing direction associated with the 360Â° video; and control presentation of the 360Â° video on the display based on the user input. The display device of Claim 6, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to present, on the display, one or more symbols identifying at least one position of desired content in the 360Â° video relative to a current viewing direction associated with the 360Â° video. The display device of Claim 6, wherein: the at least one processor is configured to control the presentation of the 360Â° video using one of: an absolute mode or a relative mode; in the absolute mode, changes to the first viewing direction are made with reference to a fixed viewing direction; and in the relative mode, changes to the first viewing direction are made with reference to a current viewing direction. The display device of Claim 6, wherein the user input comprises information relating to the first viewing direction associated with the 360Â° video and a second viewing direction associated with the 360Â° video. A method for controlling presentation of a 360Â° video on a display device, the method comprising: displaying a dynamic user interface on a touchscreen display of an electronic device, the dynamic user interface comprising a first input region forming a dial, the first input region configured to receive user input related to a first viewing direction associated with the 360Â° video; detecting the user input; and transmitting the user input to the display device to control the presentation of the 360Â° video on the display device. The method of Claim 10, wherein: the dynamic user interface further comprises a dynamic heat map; and the dynamic heat map indicates at least one position of desired content in the 360Â° video. The method of Claim 11, wherein the dynamic heat map is based on at least one of: information from a creator or provider of the 360Â° video identifying the at least one position of desired content in the 360Â° video; or information identifying viewing directions commonly used by multiple users when viewing the 360Â° video. The method of Claim 10, wherein: the dynamic user interface further comprises a second input region, the second input region configured to receive second user input related to a second viewing direction associated with the 360Â° video; and the method further comprises: detecting the second user input; and transmitting the second user input to the display device to further control the presentation of the 360Â° video on the display device.. A method for presenting a 360Â° video on a display device, the method comprising: receiving user input from a dynamic user interface displayed on a touchscreen of an electronic device, the user input related to a viewing direction associated with the 360Â° video; and controlling presentation of the 360Â° video on the display based on the user input. A computer readable medium containing instructions that when executed by at least one processor of an electronic device cause the at least one processor to perform the method of any one of claims 10 - 14.
